CNA navigation

Singapore PM announces new housing scheme

By Staff Writer · 11 May 2026 09:30 AM

SINGAPORE: Prime Minister Lawrence Wong on Monday (May 11) announced a major new housing scheme aimed at helping younger Singaporeans get on the property ladder, with a particular focus on first-time buyers under the age of 35. Speaking at a community event in the Tampines GRC, the prime minister said the scheme would offer expanded grants, longer loan terms, and access to a new category of subsidised flats.

The Housing and Development Board (HDB) will roll out 12,000 new units under the scheme over the next two years, on top of its standard build-to-order pipeline. Eligible buyers will receive grants of up to S$80,000 depending on household income, and a new five-year grace period on certain repayment milestones for couples with at least one child.

Economists have given the scheme a cautiously positive reception. "The targeting toward younger households is sensible," said Dr Lim Wei-Ming, an analyst at the Institute of Policy Studies. "But the supply expansion is the key variable. Grants alone do not solve a structural housing market problem."

The Workers' Party welcomed the focus on younger buyers, but raised concerns about whether the supply expansion would be sufficient given continuing demand from new permanent residents and citizens forming households. Party leaders have called for additional measures including a review of HDB resale price guidance.

Construction industry representatives said they expected the scheme to provide a near-term boost to demand for skilled trades, though some flagged ongoing concerns about labour availability. The scheme takes effect in July, with the first applications expected to open in early August.
